title: sorbus torminalis var. semitorminalis
intro: Sorbus torminalis var. semitorminalis, also known as the small whitebeam, is a variant of the mountain ash tree, native to the European Alps. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Sorbus torminalis var. semitorminalis are said to help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats, making it a valuable remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and other health conditions.
